Name: Bug Detection and Preparation (Triage)
Description: Initial phase focusing on identifying the failure symptom and setting up the necessary environment for investigation.

Name: Develop Test Cases and Scenarios
Description: Creation of specific, failing test cases designed to challenge the system's known failure points and boundary conditions until the bug can be consistently triggered.

Name: Set Up Debugging and Monitoring Environment
Description: Configuration and validation of specialized tools (debuggers, logging frameworks, profilers) and hardware simulators required for deep runtime observation.

Name: Reproduce the Failure (Confirmation)
Description: Systematic execution of the failure-triggering test case in a controlled environment until the bug is consistently observed, capturing the precise sequence of events.

Name: Deep Technical Investigation (Diagnosis)
Description: The core technical process involving multiple methods to trace data flow, memory allocation, and execution paths at the point of failure.

Name: Code Inspection and Root Cause Analysis (RCA)
Description: Analyzing the code surrounding the failure point using logic review, manual inspection (code review), and formal techniques (e.g., Fault Tree Analysis) to determine the fundamental cause.

Name: Develop and Implement Mitigation Patch
Description: Developing the minimal, robust code changes necessary to correct the root cause. Requires peer review prior to commit.

Name: Unit Test and Fix Validation
Description: Executing highly targeted unit tests (TDD approach) to prove that the patch successfully resolves the initial failure, ideally in isolation.

Name: Full Regression and System Validation
Description: Executing the entire suite of existing system tests, integration tests, and performance tests to ensure that the fix did not introduce any new side effects or regressions.

Name: Documentation and Process Update
Description: Finalizing the process by updating the knowledge base, revising design documents, and implementing architectural changes or preventative checks to ensure the bug never reappears.
